scope.platform: macOS Foundation decision; Windows is a future reopen condition

method.authority: Framework maintainers, platform vendors, and tool vendors only
method.v1_salvage: Only sources.json and paper-screen.md at 8684b68 were inspected as untrusted search leads.
method.revalidation: Every retained URL was opened again against its current or pinned official publication.
exclusions: No v1 prose, source code, measurements, binaries, Windows results, or fingerprint is evidence.
A missing official route is recorded as an evidence gap, not proof that the capability is impossible.
This index contains no Tauri-versus-Slint prototype result or recommendation.

sources.publisher: Tauri Programme within The Commons Conservancy
sources.title: Capabilities | Tauri
claims: Capabilities grant or deny permissions by window or webview.
Application commands require explicit AppManifest restriction for a closed command surface.

sources.publisher: Tauri Programme within The Commons Conservancy
sources.title: WebDriver | Tauri
claims: WebdriverIO's Tauri service documents Windows, Linux, and macOS support.
The free macOS route embeds a WebDriver server in the tested application; direct tauri-driver omits macOS.
The embedded plugin must therefore be proved absent from a release-like package.

sources.publisher: Tauri Programme within The Commons Conservancy
sources.title: Updater | Tauri
claims: The updater supports macOS and requires signatures for update verification.
Update signing cannot be disabled.

sources.publisher: Tauri Programme within The Commons Conservancy
sources.title: macOS Code Signing | Tauri
claims: Tauri documents Developer ID signing and Apple notarization inputs for macOS distribution.

sources.publisher: Tauri Programme within The Commons Conservancy
sources.title: Tauri workspace Cargo manifest
claims: The pinned Tauri workspace declares MIT OR Apache-2.0 licensing.

sources.title: Desktop | Slint Docs
claims: Slint documents tested Apple Silicon macOS 14, 15, and 26 desktop configurations.
Slint also documents Windows desktop configurations for the later reopen.

sources.title: Common Properties & Callbacks | Slint Docs
claims: Slint exposes roles, labels, descriptions, actions, landmarks, and stable accessibility identifiers.

sources.title: Cargo Features | slint
claims: The system-testing feature permits remote UI introspection and control.
Slint labels system-testing a developer/testing feature and does not recommend it for production builds.

sources.title: Slint Royalty-free Desktop, Mobile, and Web Applications License
claims: The royalty-free grant covers distribution as part of a desktop application.
Distribution requires either the AboutSlint widget in an About or splash surface, or an easily found attribution badge on a public webpage.

sources.title: Sparkle 2 Documentation
claims: Sparkle documents a separate macOS updater framework with Ed25519 update signatures.
Using it would add a native framework, packaging, signing, key-management, and lifecycle boundary; it is not a Slint-owned integrated updater.

sources.title: XCTest
claims: XCTest with XCUIAutomation can interact with application UI and validate user flows.

sources.title: Notarizing macOS software before distribution
claims: Developer ID-signed macOS software can be submitted to Apple's notary service.
Successful notarization produces a ticket that can be stapled to supported distribution artifacts.

sources.title: GitHub-hosted runners reference
claims: GitHub documents standard macos-14 and macos-26 runners as Apple Silicon M1 arm64.
Workflow logs remain the authority for the exact image used by an evidence run.

sources.title: Supported Platforms | Qt 6.11
claims: Qt documents arm64 macOS and x86-64 Windows desktop targets.
Platform support can vary by module and licence.

sources.title: Qt Licensing | Qt 6.11
claims: Qt offers commercial and LGPLv3/GPL routes with module-specific exceptions.
A module-level legal and redistribution inventory is required.

sources.title: Accessibility for Qt Quick Applications | Qt 6.11
claims: Qt Quick controls expose keyboard behavior and accessibility metadata.
Custom QML items require explicit accessible roles, names, and actions.

sources.title: Qt Quick Test | Qt 6.11
claims: Qt Quick Test is an in-process unit-test framework for QML applications.
It does not by itself establish external packaged-shell or native-dialog automation.

sources.title: Overview of Qt Installer Framework
claims: Qt Installer Framework documents installer and maintenance-tool construction.

sources.title: Introduction | CXX-Qt Documentation
claims: CXX-Qt generates a C++ representation and CXX bridge from Rust annotations.
Its documented CI covers x86-64 Windows and macOS, not Apple Silicon.

sources.title: GPUI README
claims: GPUI documents macOS Metal and Windows Win32/DirectWrite platform routes.
GPUI is pre-1.0, frequently breaking, and directs consumers to source code for much of the API.

sources.title: GPUI Cargo manifest
claims: The GPUI crate declares Apache-2.0 licensing.

sources.title: Desktop | Dioxus 0.7
claims: Dioxus Desktop uses a system WebView while Rust code runs natively.
Raw JavaScript and bidirectional WebView messaging are available through document evaluation.

sources.title: Bundle | Dioxus 0.7
claims: Dioxus documents desktop bundle generation through its CLI.

sources.title: Testing and Debugging | Dioxus 0.7
claims: Official end-to-end guidance points to Playwright browser testing.
The guidance does not establish packaged desktop or native-dialog automation.

sources.title: Dioxus MIT License
claims: Dioxus v0.7.0 is distributed under MIT terms.

sources.title: Desktop support for Flutter
claims: Flutter documents native Windows and macOS desktop builds and release builds.

sources.title: Bind to native code using the legacy FFI plugin template
claims: Flutter desktop applications can call native C APIs through dart:ffi.
Current guidance prefers the package_ffi template over the legacy plugin_ffi template.

sources.title: Testing plugins
claims: Dart integration_test cannot interact with native UI such as native dialogs.
Flutter points to a native UI framework or another tool when native UI interaction is required.

sources.title: Accessibility
claims: Flutter documents framework semantics and an accessibility release checklist.
The cited checklist is not macOS VoiceOver or IME acceptance evidence.

sources.title: Flutter BSD 3-Clause License
claims: Flutter 3.44.0 is distributed under BSD 3-Clause terms.

sources.title: Get started with WinUI
claims: WinUI 3 is a Windows-only native desktop UI framework supporting C# and C++.

sources.title: UI Automation Overview
claims: Microsoft UI Automation exposes desktop UI elements to assistive tools and automated tests.

sources.title: What is MSIX?
claims: MSIX provides Windows packaging, signed integrity, install and uninstall, and update routes.

sources.title: SwiftUI
claims: SwiftUI is Apple's declarative application UI framework.

sources.title: Window | winit
claims: winit provides cross-platform window and event-loop plumbing.
IME is opt-in and requires the consumer to manage IME events and candidate-window geometry.

sources.title: wgpu
claims: wgpu provides native Metal and DirectX 12 graphics backends.
wgpu is a graphics API, not a widget, accessibility, automation, or updater platform.

sources.title: accesskit_winit
claims: The winit adapter depends on platform adapters for macOS and Windows.
The consumer still owns the complete semantic tree and widget behavior.

evidence_gaps.statement: No Slint-owned integrated signed updater was identified in the official Slint desktop, platform, feature, or licensing documentation inspected on 2026-07-17. Sparkle is a possible separate macOS recipe, not evidence that Slint satisfies the issue's signed-update win-gate; the evaluation therefore records this as a Slint failure rather than an unresolved environmental stop.

Royalty-free License 2.0 has an explicit attribution condition. The prototype implemented no reviewed compliant attribution route, so Slint fails the evaluation licence gate; any future reconsideration still requires legal review.

The documented system-testing feature exposes remote UI control and is not recommended for production. The governed release-like package scan proved that the feature, environment-triggered connection path, and test server were absent; the exact proof is retained in the benchmark evidence map.
